one_time_description: Large CMHC funding of $4,059,526 including Rapid Housing Initiative ($2,100,388 deferred) representing a significant non-recurring capital housing project
economic_notes: First Nation engaged in social, cultural and economic development. Alamos Gold Trust fund present ($112,977). New CMHC construction project undertaken in the year. Economic dependence on Indigenous Services Canada and Health Canada noted.
notes: Audit disclaimer of opinion issued (not a qualified opinion but a disclaimer) due to missing Health segment data for first 3 months, missing supporting documentation for first 8 months, and undocumented CMHC construction project. Fiscal year is 2022 not 2024. Remuneration schedule not included in the financial statements; Chief and Council honoraria of $221,339 noted in expenses by object. Advances from CMHC of $382,160 appear on balance sheet. Current liabilities include accounts payable $4,197,191, government remittances payable $124,155, and advances from CMHC $382,160 (partially obscured in source). Houses cost column shows negative disposal of ($1,222,469) transferred to construction in progress. OSR segment revenue of $663,272 plus ISETS other revenue $15,318 and other departmental non-government revenue makes up OSR total.
